zh.lifeSummary: Lady Fiona Stewart of Atholl是Walter Stewart Earl of Athol, brother of King Robert II的女兒，於1347年出生在蘇格蘭登巴頓史爾馮 藍妮克絲。她於大約1373年與Ranald Reginald MacDonald 1st MacDonald of ClanRanald and 1st of Glengarry在蘇格蘭結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1390年在蘇格蘭音巴內-爾莫達爾特享年43歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Lady Fiona Stewart of Atholl是Walter Stewart Earl of Athol, brother of King Robert II的女儿，于1347年出生在苏格兰登巴頓史爾馮 藍妮克絲。她于大约1373年与Ranald Reginald MacDonald 1st MacDonald of ClanRanald and 1st of Glengarry在苏格兰结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1390年在苏格兰音巴內-爾莫達爾特享年43岁。

nameMeanings.name: Stewart
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish (Lanarkshire) and English: originally an occupational name for an administrative official of an estate, from Middle English stiward, Old English stigweard, stīweard, a compound of stig ‘house(hold)’ + weard ‘guardian’. In the Anglo-Saxon period this title was used of an officer controlling the domestic affairs of a household, especially of the royal household; after the Norman Conquest it was also used more widely as the native equivalent of Seneschal, for the steward of a manor or manager of an estate. In Scotland the term was also used of a magistrate originally appointed by the king to administer crown lands, forming a stewartry.

History: Stuart or Stewart is the surname of one of the great families of Scotland, the royal family of Scotland from the 14th century, and of England from 1603, when James VI of Scotland acceded to the English throne as James I. There were many minor branches of the family left in Britain after the flight of James II in 1688, but not every bearer of the surname can claim relationship with the royal house, even in Scotland. Every great house in medieval England and Scotland had its steward, and in many cases the office gave rise to a hereditary surname. The fall of the house of Stuart in Britain, conversely, led to the establishment of several highly placed branches bearing this surname in continental Europe, which are in most cases related to the old Scottish royal family.

variants.name: Stuart
meanings: Scottish (Aberdeenshire and Banffshire): variant of   Stewart  . This French form of the name, there being no W in the French language, was adopted by Mary, Queen of Scots, on her return to Scotland in 1561 and has since come to be more popular among royalists than the earlier spellings.
History: Stuart or Stewart is the surname of one of the great families of Scotland (see note at   Stewart  ).

variants.name: Stewardson
meanings: English (Lancashire and Westmorland): patronymic from Middle English stiward ‘steward’ (see Stewart ) + -son ‘son of the steward’.

variants.name: Steward
meanings: English (London and East Anglia): variant of   Stewart  .

nameMeanings.name: Fiona
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: derivative of the Gaelic word fionn ‘white, fair’. It was first used by James Macpherson ( 1736–96 ), author of the Ossianic poems, which were supposedly translations from ancient Gaelic. It was subsequently used as a pen-name by William Sharp ( 1855–1905 ), who produced many romantic works under the name of Fiona Macleod , and has since become popular throughout the English-speaking world.

en.lifeSummary: Lady Fiona Stewart of Atholl was born in 1347, in The Lennox, Dunbartonshire, Scotland as the daughter of Walter Stewart Earl of Athol, brother of King Robert II. She married Ranald Reginald MacDonald 1st MacDonald of ClanRanald and 1st of Glengarry about 1373, in Scotland. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 1 daughter. She died in 1390, in Moidart, Inverness-shire, Scotland, at the age of 43.

fr.lifeSummary: Lady Fiona Stewart of Atholl est née en 1347, à The Lennox, Dunbartonshire, Écosse, fille de Walter Stewart Earl of Athol, brother of King Robert II. Elle a épousé Ranald Reginald MacDonald 1st MacDonald of ClanRanald and 1st of Glengarry environ 1373, à Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée en 1390, à Moidart, Inverness-shire, Écosse, à l’âge de 43 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Lady Fiona Stewart of Atholl wurde 1347 in The Lennox, Dunbartonshire, Scotland, als Tochter von Walter Stewart Earl of Athol, brother of King Robert II geboren. Sie hat um 1373 in Scotland Ranald Reginald MacDonald 1st MacDonald of ClanRanald and 1st of Glengarry geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb 1390 im Alter von 43 Jahren in Moidart, Inverness-shire, Scotland.

ja.lifeSummary: Lady Fiona Stewart of Athollは1347年にスコットランドダンバートンシュアイアス レノックスでWalter Stewart Earl of Athol, brother of King Robert IIの娘として生まれた。約1373年にスコットランドでRanald Reginald MacDonald 1st MacDonald of ClanRanald and 1st of Glengarryと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1390年にスコットランドインバーネス-シュアイアモイダルトで43歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Fiona Stewart of Atholl(은)는 1347년 스컷랜드 던바토실르 테 렌스에서 Walter Stewart Earl of Athol, brother of King Robert II의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 대략 1373년 스컷랜드에서 Ranald Reginald MacDonald 1st MacDonald of ClanRanald and 1st of Glengarry(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1390년 스컷랜드 인버네스-실르 모이더르트에서 43세의 나이로 사망했다.

Ela casou-se com Ranald Reginald MacDonald 1st MacDonald of ClanRanald and 1st of Glengarry aproximadamente 1373, em Scotland. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 1 filha. ru.lifeSummary: Lady Fiona Stewart of Atholl родилась в 1347 г. в местности т леннокс, дунбартоншир, Шотландия. Её отца звали Walter Stewart Earl of Athol, brother of King Robert II. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Ranald Reginald MacDonald 1st MacDonald of ClanRanald and 1st of Glengarry около 1373 г. в местности Шотландия. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 1 дочь. Она умерла в 1390 г. в местности моидарт, инвирнесс-шир, Шотландия в возрасте 43 лет.
